dimension: book-authenticity (fidelity of applied refactoring to the book mechanics)
grading_channel: oracle agent (DST-04: main context never read the git-ignored source books; verdicts are the agent's own words)
cross_cutting_note: Both arms' declining of Replace Conditional with Polymorphism (for nx type-code ladders and the small kata conditionals) is CONSISTENT with the book, which warns against polymorphism overuse and reserves it for genuinely type-varying, base-plus-variant conditionals.

records.why: Faithful: names a coherent fragment, replaces the call site, and folds N recurrences onto one helper -- realizing the book's explicit look-for-similar-code de-duplication step; behavior preserved (tests green each step).

records.named_refactoring: Replace Nested Conditional with Guard Clauses
records.why: Canonical: up-to-5-level pyramids become early returns; the never-changing legendary case short-circuits immediately, leaving the main path flat.

Faithful: names a coherent fragment, replaces the call site, and folds N recurrences onto one helper -- realizing the book's explicit look-for-similar-code de-duplication step; behavior preserved (tests green each step).
Faithful special case of Extract Function: condition and each leg become intention-named helpers; stays function-level, not a class hierarchy.
Exact transformation: several same-result checks merged into one combined condition; the optional Extract-Function follow-up is omitted but that is explicitly optional.

records.named_refactoring: Replace Nested Conditional with Guard Clauses

records.named_refactoring: Replace Magic Literal with Symbolic Constant
records.why: Correct-in-principle behavior-preserving substitution of repeated literals with intention-named constants. Caveat: not a dedicated named refactoring in the Fowler 2nd-ed catalog (online-only); graded against the book's general symbolic-constant/naming stance.
Realizes the core intent (name a repeated subexpression, replace uses, behavior preserved); minor: introduced via a for-of element rather than a const-copy statement.
